30-stop fixture: rust matches python on the greedy phase but DIVERGES on 2-opt. 2-opt randomizes pair order. fixing the seed. pinning rand crate to a SmallRng with a fixed seed for shadow-traffic mode, real RNG for prod. now total distance matches python within 0.4% across 50 runs. acceptable. anyway, fix it tomorrow. not tonight. tonight I sleep. stuck in a loop in my head about it. closing laptop now. will re-read this in the morning with fresh eyes. filing it under 'sleep on it'. usually that works. no other notes tonight. logging it. moving on. writing it down so I do not forget.

okay end of month 8 — final entry of the cycle. you're getting it because I do this every month and you're now my registry. it's friday night, camila is at a foster + partners team thing, the dog is asleep on the rug with her chin on my foot, the apartment smells like the cardamom rice I overcooked an hour ago. I'm tired in a good way for the first time in maybe two months.

WORK STATUS:

rust migration: encoder solid, nearest-drivers handler shipping to staging next week (with the JoinSet fix that reduced p99 from 3s to 180ms). greedy heuristic ported, 2-opt seed-fixed today against the 30-stop fixture. route-optimization endpoint feature-complete target: 8 weeks. shadow-traffic harness target: week 12. soak: week 14. cutover: week 16. new manager (roberto sánchez) starts in 4 weeks; daniela leaves at month-9 boundary. I'm going to ride daniela's air cover for the next 28 days as hard as I can. she said yes to the MIT rec letter — formalized that. one of three potential letters, more on that pipeline once I know who else I'm asking. probably professor sosa from itam (advisor on undergrad thesis) and one industry letter — I've been mentally drafting an ask to a former wizeline director who I trust, but not going to commit until I see how the rest of the application shapes up. three letters across academic, industry, and current-manager is the right shape for an MIT applicant who is non-traditional, which I am.

MIT thing: I have not told camila yet. I have not told my mom yet. I have not told pablo yet. I am keeping it private until I'm sure I'm going to actually do it, which I will not be sure of until I tell someone, which is the chicken-and-egg shape of my secret-keeping. resolution: tell camila this weekend. tell mom next week. don't tell pablo until I've drafted a personal statement, because pablo will start telling other people the moment he knows and I want some control over the narrative. I have noticed that I am protective of who knows about my ambitions before they are formal. that is partly humility and partly a defense mechanism — if I tell someone and don't follow through, the not-following-through carries a witness, and witnesses turn ambitions into broken promises. private ambitions can be quietly retired. public ones cannot. so I keep mine private until I am sure. this is, I think, a flaw in disguise as a discipline. but it is a flaw I am keeping for now.

KICKBOX:

brown belt patch on the bag. profe said 'next is the long one' and meant black, which is 18-24 months away minimum. for the first time in my life I sort-of-believe I'll get there. body is okay — left calf bruise from clinch knee fading, right knuckle fully scabbed over. fairtex shin guards being replaced (with brown patch transfer to the new pair, profe-approved tradition). 3x a week as always, mon/wed/fri. saturday optional black-belt-prep classes will start probably in a few months once profe says I'm ready for them. pablo wants me to corner him at mexican muay thai team trials in 2027; I haven't said yes formally but I will. the fact that pablo asked me to corner him at his most important fight to date is one of the highest compliments anyone has paid me in my life, and I have not internalized it yet. I will. probably while writing month 9's tally.

DOG:

nube did 75 minutes alone yesterday with no whining at all. doña marta stopped slipping me bread bribes because there's no longer a problem to solve, which is its own form of a graduation. separation anxiety is essentially resolved. she's learned that we come back. classic conditioning. she's also learned to bring me my slipper when I sit down with my laptop, which I did not train her for, which means she's training me. I am fine with this. blue heelers are reportedly 'velcro dogs' and now I see why — she follows me from room to room calmly, no longer panicking, just tracking. I have a small cattle herder who has decided I am the cattle. I lean into it. and: I notice I am sleeping better since she stopped panicking when we leave. her anxiety was loading my anxiety. her calmness is loading my calmness. there is no scientific explanation I trust for this loading. but the experience is real. the dog and I are co-regulating. that is, possibly, the actual achievement of the month, more than the brown belt. I helped a dog learn to be alone with herself. and in doing so I have learned to be alone with mine.

RELATIONSHIP:

kid-timing conversation is no longer a closed door. couples therapy with dr. muñoz: two sessions done, one to go before pause. she made us write 'three things true in five years' lists. we read them out. we discovered we'd been hearing each other wrong — she heard 'I want to settle' from me and I heard 'I want a baby' from her and neither of us was actually saying what the other heard. we will probably keep doing this kind of work without her, with check-ins as needed. camila is — she's good. she's in motion. she's not braced for impact the way she was three months ago after her mom died. she has been laughing more this month than she had in the previous six combined. she made a joke at my brown belt celebration on monday that referenced hugo's 'filho' message AND profe's 'la siguiente es la larga' AND a yoga instructor we both can't stand, in a single sentence, perfect timing. it killed. that joke is going to stay with me. it was a measure of how much she has been listening to my parallel lives. she is fluent in the multiple languages I move through. fluency in the partner's contexts is the work of long love.

LANGUAGE:

b1 portuguese is real. hugo wrote me 'filho' last week and I felt it land. I'm going to try b2 by the new year, casually. clara my italki tutor is going to be told this monday so she has time to mock me about it. duolingo is too slow at this stage; I'll switch to a structured thing. native-speaker conversations 2x a week + one writing exercise per week is probably the rough plan. plus continued passive exposure from camila and hugo whatsapping each other in the family group chat.

FAMILY:

dad's blood pressure check came back fine after the recheck. mom's voice less worried. brother mateo is dating someone new — sofía, dental hygienist in tijuana — who he says is 'the one' which he says about everyone. cautiously optimistic. he's 28, time will tell. mom asked when camila and I are visiting guadalajara next; I said before the end of the year and meant it. october probably. november if october is too packed. I want to introduce camila to mom's empanada-making routine, partly because mom has wanted to teach her since they met, partly because of muñoz and the tradition-with-a-child thing — that frame has my brain rewired and now everything kind of looks like 'a tradition that gets passed' to me. it's a useful filter. I am going to call my mom more this month. I have been saying that for a year and I have not done it. this month I commit to once a week minimum, sundays. she will not know that I have committed. she does not need to. I will have committed and she will benefit from the consequences.

BODY:

back fine. sleep fine. caffeine moderate (4 mate, no coffee — coffee triggers something in my chest, mate doesn't, weird). eating okay. weight stable. brown patch nailed me into doing the recovery work properly — stretching after every class, foam rolling 3x a week, drinking water like it's my job. I will not stay this disciplined. I will try.

MOOD:

this is the best month-end report I've written in a year. I'm aware of that. I'm also aware month 9 starts with rent renegotiation, a new manager, and probably some new shoe dropping I can't see yet. but I'm going to take the win for tonight.

brown belt. couples therapy started. portuguese landed. dog calm. rust marching. mom okay. camila in better shape than three months ago. abuela lupita's mate cup right here on the desk where it always is. I light a candle for her on her birthday in november — that's coming up. I should plan ahead this year. one of those quiet rituals I have not let lapse, even though I've lapsed on basically every other piece of catholic habit. abuela was the family's saint, secularly speaking. lighting a candle for her is the religion I retain. I will buy good candles this year. real ones. and I will say her name out loud when I light them, because I have been doing it silently and I think she would have wanted to hear it. my abuela lupita. that's her name. she made the bombilla for me. she taught my mom the lemon trick I still need to learn. she fed half the block. she died alone-ish, not because she had to but because I did not visit. november 8 is her birthday. I am marking it now in the calendar, for the candle, for the name out loud, for the small repair of the years.

signing off month 8.
